stocks.name: Micron Technology Inc.
stocks.explanation: UBS nearly tripled its price target on Micron to $1,625 from $535, citing long-term AI memory supply agreements that stabilize historically volatile earnings. The upgrade drove shares up 18% to breach a $1 trillion market cap for the first time. UBS argued there is no reason Micron should trade at a discount to Nvidia given its AI-driven demand visibility.

stocks.name: AppLovin Corporation
stocks.explanation: AppLovin continued its rally as anticipation builds for the June worldwide opening of its AXON advertising platform to all advertisers, closing a 14-year chapter as a closed system. The company's Q1 revenue grew 59% YoY to $1.84 billion, beating estimates. Analyst notes suggest the perceived risk from Meta competition has faded.

stocks.name: ON Semiconductor Corp.
stocks.explanation: ON Semiconductor hit a 52-week high as the broader semiconductor rally lifted power-focused chipmakers. The stock has surged over 140% in the past year on cyclical semiconductor recovery and surging data center power demand. AI infrastructure buildouts from hyperscalers are driving massive demand for ON's power management chips.

stocks.name: Unity Software Inc.
stocks.explanation: Unity Software rose as a beneficiary of AppLovin's AXON platform opening, which analysts say simplifies the competitive landscape for both companies. The mobile gaming and real-time 3D engine maker has been recovering from its 2024 restructuring, with renewed focus on its core gaming and digital twin businesses.

stocks.name: Monolithic Power Systems Inc.
stocks.explanation: Monolithic Power Systems gained alongside the semiconductor sector rally, with AI data center power delivery driving strong demand for its high-performance analog and mixed-signal ICs. The company's server power solutions are increasingly critical as AI racks consume hundreds of kilowatts per unit.

stocks.name: Qualcomm Inc.
stocks.explanation: Qualcomm surged to a new intraday record after Bloomberg reported the chipmaker struck a deal with ByteDance to supply AI data-center ASICs. The partnership makes ByteDance one of Qualcomm's first major data-center customers, marking a significant expansion beyond mobile processors. ByteDance boosted its AI infrastructure budget 25% to $29.4 billion.

stocks.name: KLA Corporation
stocks.explanation: KLA Corporation advanced in the semiconductor equipment rally as strong AI chip demand drives capital expenditure increases across foundries and memory manufacturers. The company's process control and yield management systems are critical to manufacturing advanced AI chips at scale.

stocks.name: Microchip Technology Inc.
stocks.explanation: Microchip Technology rose as the analog and embedded chip sector benefited from broad semiconductor strength. The company has been recovering from inventory destocking, with automotive and industrial end markets stabilizing alongside growing AI edge deployment opportunities.

stocks.name: Analog Devices Inc.
stocks.explanation: Analog Devices gained as the chip sector broadly rallied on AI spending momentum. ADI's precision signal processing and power management solutions are seeing increased demand from data center, automotive, and industrial automation customers investing in AI capabilities.

stocks.name: Wolfspeed Inc.
stocks.explanation: Wolfspeed extended its remarkable May rally, up over 100% this month after Citrini Research named it a top AI infrastructure pick. The thesis centers on next-generation AI racks requiring complete power conversion redesigns using silicon carbide — Wolfspeed's specialty. The company produced its first 300mm silicon carbide crystal in January 2026.

stocks.name: Advanced Micro Devices Inc.
stocks.explanation: AMD rose alongside the broader chip sector rally, continuing to benefit from AI GPU demand and data center expansion. The company's MI series accelerators compete for AI training and inference workloads, with hyperscaler customers increasingly diversifying beyond Nvidia.

stocks.name: Texas Instruments Inc.
stocks.explanation: Texas Instruments advanced as analog semiconductor demand strengthened across automotive, industrial, and data center applications. TI's massive manufacturing expansion positions it to capture growing analog chip demand driven by AI infrastructure and electrification trends.

stocks.name: C3.ai Inc.
stocks.explanation: C3.ai shares rose as AI-related stocks broadly rallied on semiconductor momentum and continued enterprise AI adoption. The enterprise AI software provider has been expanding its generative AI offerings and government contracts.

stocks.name: MongoDB Inc.
stocks.explanation: MongoDB declined ahead of its earnings report scheduled for May 28, with investors taking profits after a recent recovery. The database company faces questions about growth deceleration and competition from AI-native database solutions, though its Atlas cloud platform crossed a $2 billion annualized run rate.

stocks.name: Applied Materials Inc.
stocks.explanation: Applied Materials gained as semiconductor equipment stocks rallied on strong AI chip demand driving fab investment. AMAT's etch, deposition, and inspection tools are essential to producing advanced logic and memory chips powering AI workloads.
